title: Manual transaction entry + Transactions list
description:
Transactions tab: list of imported/manual transactions with fixed/variable tagging, manual entry flow, and MerchantRule management. Replaces the Today screen's `AddTransactionStubSheet` stub and the `RootTabView` "Transactions" placeholder `Text`. Depends only on data models (reservoir-adq.1, done); does not require the calculator.

## Scope note: retroactive re-tagging now lives in this story (2026-07-07 decision)

JP decided MerchantRule create/edit **does** retroactively re-tag existing matching transactions (see comment log). That means the core merchant-match engine is needed immediately by this story's own MerchantRule CRUD UI, not just by adq.4's import-time flow. Scope split:

- **This story (adq.3) owns**: the merchant-name-match function (`[MerchantRule] -> String -> TransactionType?`) as a pure, testable `Services/` type, AND both of its current call sites: (1) the manual-entry form's auto-suggest, (2) the retroactive retag pass triggered synchronously on MerchantRule create/edit.
- **adq.4 owns**: applying that same function at *import time* only (when Plaid delivers new transactions, future bead reservoir-adq.6+). adq.4 does not rebuild the matching engine — it depends on this story's `Services/` type and calls it. See adq.4's description for its narrowed scope.

This is option (a) from the reconciliation: pull the matching engine into adq.3 since MerchantRule CRUD needs it now, leave adq.4 as a thin import-time consumer of the same function. Rationale: building the match function twice (or building it here and having adq.4 duplicate it) would violate STANDARDS §3 (no copy-paste) and risk the two call sites drifting into different matching semantics (e.g. one going case-insensitive-exact, the other creeping toward substring match) — one shared implementation forecloses that.

**Known open issue, deliberately deferred (not dropped):** the manual-entry form's auto-suggest and the retroactive-retag-on-rule-save pass both call the same matching function, but *how* they're wired (do they literally call one shared function today, or do they happen to implement the same logic twice for now with a TODO to unify) is JP's call to make later, not resolved in this story. Engineer should implement one shared pure function and have both call sites use it — that satisfies both the immediate need and leaves nothing to unify later. Flagging only because JP explicitly said not to silently drop the question.

## Data model (already in place — SchemaV2, no changes needed for this story)

- `SpendTransaction`: `amount`, `date`, `merchantName`, `type` (variable/fixed), `entryMethod` (manual/imported), `plaidTransactionID` (nil for manual), `isManualOverride` (Bool — true once a user hand-sets/changes `type`, so future MerchantRule re-application doesn't clobber it), `savingsGoal` (optional `SavingsGoal?` relationship — nil is a valid "unattributed" state), `createdAt`.
- `MerchantRule`: `merchantName` (exact, case-insensitive match), `type` (variable/fixed). **No goal-attribution field, and none is being added.** (2026-07-12 resolution: JP and orchestrator settled the former "open question 3" — no `defaultGoal` field, no SchemaV3, goal attribution stays manual/per-transaction for MVP. Rationale: attribution only matters once 2+ SavingsGoals are concurrently active with independent carry-forwards, and PROJECT_SPEC.md marks multi-goal support Phase 2, not MVP. See NOTES for full reasoning. This is settled, not open — do not resurface during implementation.)
- `SavingsGoal`: unchanged, used only for the goal-attribution picker.

## UX / requirements

### Manual transaction entry (add + edit, same form)
Fields:
- **Amount** — required, numeric keypad, must be > 0 (existing validation rule, spec §"Empty states and validation").
- **Date** — defaults to today; editable via date picker. Bound: **must be today or in the past**, not future. Reasoning: a transaction represents money already spent; a future-dated entry would silently inflate "spent today"/carry-forward math on a day that hasn't happened yet, corrupting the core mechanic's trust (PROJECT_SPEC "Core mechanic" — this is the same trust concern that made dedup a Phase-1, not deferred, concern). No lower bound — backdating a cash purchase from days ago is a legitimate fallback use case per the spec's "needed as fallback even with Plaid."
- **Merchant / description** — required free-text field, non-empty after trimming whitespace.
- **Type (variable/fixed)** — segmented control, defaults to `variable`. If the entered merchant name exact-matches an existing `MerchantRule` (case-insensitive), auto-select that rule's `type` when the merchant field loses focus/on save, but let the user override it in the same form. If the user changes the type away from what the rule would set, mark `isManualOverride = true` on save (this is what protects the entry from later auto-retag passes). If no rule matches, default `variable` and `isManualOverride = false`. This auto-suggest uses the same merchant-match function as the retroactive retag pass below (see scope note above).
- **Goal attribution** — picker of active `SavingsGoal`s (see `TodayScreenCalculator.activeGoals`). Behavior: if exactly one active goal exists, pre-select it (common case, minimize taps for the target user). If zero or more than one active goal exists, default to **unattributed** and require the user to either pick a goal or explicitly confirm "unattributed" — do not silently guess among multiple goals, since misattribution feeds directly into the wrong goal's carry-forward. Unattributed is always a legal end state (e.g. no goal exists yet, or the spend genuinely isn't tied to any goal) — the transaction still saves and still counts toward the day's total spend.
  - **Zero-active-goals case, clarified 2026-07-12** (flagged by product review pass): the Today screen's "Add transaction" entry is unreachable with zero goals (Today shows a goal-creation prompt instead), but the Transactions tab's own independent "+" entry point *is* reachable with zero goals. In that state the goal picker has no real choices — it should render as "Unattributed" pre-selected and effectively a no-op confirm (no goal list to pick from, just an acknowledged unattributed state), not an empty/broken-looking picker. This is a UI-state clarification, not a scope change.
- **Category** — **not a field.** Not in the data model (PROJECT_SPEC's `Transaction` table has no category column) and not asked for anywhere in the spec's UX section. Adding one here would be scope creep beyond what the spec locked in.

Entry point: the existing "Add transaction" button on the Today screen (`TodayView.isShowingAddTransaction`, currently `StubSheet`) now presents this real form instead of the stub. The Transactions tab also gets its own "+" entry point (toolbar button) for adding a transaction without going through Today — both present the same `TransactionEntryView` sheet.

Editing: tapping a row in the Transactions list opens the same form pre-filled, including reassigning `savingsGoal` after the fact (e.g. correcting a misattributed spend) — low-stakes, no reason to lock it down once creation already allows free-form attribution. Deleting: swipe-to-delete on the list row, with a confirmation alert (irreversible; SwiftData hard-deletes) — no soft-delete/undo in MVP, matches the app's overall lack of an undo system elsewhere.

### Transactions list
- Sorted **date descending** (newest first, matching `TodayView`'s existing convention: `SortDescriptor(\.date, order: .reverse)` then `\.createdAt, order: .reverse)` as the same-day tiebreak).
- **Grouped by day** (section headers, e.g. "Today," "Yesterday," then full dates) — a flat list of amounts with no day boundary is hard to scan for a user who isn't financially sophisticated; day grouping is a low-cost SwiftUI `List`/`Section` pattern that directly supports "how much did I spend on X day."
- Each row shows: type icon (reuse `TodayView.TransactionRow`'s `lock.fill`/`cart.fill` convention), merchant name, time, amount, and a muted "Excluded from limit" caption + reduced opacity for `fixed` transactions (same visual language as the Today screen's recent-transactions section — consistency matters more than novelty here). Also show a small goal-attribution indicator (goal name or "Unattributed") since this view, unlike Today's abbreviated recent list, is where the user audits attribution.
- **Filter**: a segmented control (All / Variable / Fixed) at the top of the list. Recommended and in-scope: cheap to build (a `Picker` + local filter, no new query), and directly useful for "how much of my spending this month was fixed vs. variable" — a question this exact user persona (budget-conscious, tracking a rolling limit) will actually ask.
- **Search**: **out of scope for this story.** A single-user personal ledger with modest transaction volume doesn't yet justify a search bar; the day-grouped list is enough to scan. Revisit if transaction volume after Plaid import (adq.6) makes scanning impractical.

### MerchantRule management (lives in this tab, per spec IA)
- A "Merchant rules" list, reachable via a toolbar/nav-link from the Transactions tab (not its own top-level tab — the spec's 4-tab IA is locked).
- List shows each rule: merchant name + type tag (variable/fixed).
- **Create**: "+" button opens a form with merchant name (required, non-empty, trimmed) and type (variable/fixed, required, no default — forces an explicit choice since this is a rule that will silently tag transactions, both new and retroactively).
- **Edit**: tap a rule to change its merchant name or type.
- **Delete**: swipe-to-delete, with confirmation. Deleting a rule does **not** retroactively untag/revert previously-retagged transactions — those keep whatever `type` they were last set to. Only create/edit trigger a forward retag pass; delete is inert with respect to existing transaction data. (Asymmetric on purpose: reverting a retag would require tracking "what would this transaction's type have been without the rule," which isn't in the data model and isn't worth adding for MVP.)
- Duplicate merchant names: reject on save (case-insensitive) with an inline error — two rules for the same merchant string is an unresolvable conflict, not a case to silently allow last-write-wins.
- **Retroactive retag on create/edit (new in this story per JP's 2026-07-07 decision)**: when a MerchantRule is created, or edited such that its `merchantName` or `type` changes, immediately walk all existing `SpendTransaction`s where `merchantName` case-insensitively matches the rule's `merchantName` AND `isManualOverride == false`, and set their `type` to the rule's `type`. Transactions with `isManualOverride == true` are never touched by this pass — that flag exists specifically to protect a user's explicit choice from being silently overwritten by a later rule change. This pass runs synchronously as part of the rule save (small personal dataset, no background job needed). **What's still adq.4's job**: applying this same match function when Plaid imports new transactions (a different trigger — import, not rule CRUD — and a different bead since it depends on the not-yet-built Plaid integration, adq.6).

## Technical approach

- New feature folder `Reservoir/Features/Transactions/` (STANDARDS §4, organize by feature not layer):
  - `TransactionsView.swift` — the tab root: filter control, day-grouped `@Query`-backed list, toolbar "+" for add, nav link to merchant rules.
  - `TransactionEntryView.swift` — add/edit form, used both from `TransactionsView`'s "+" and from `TodayView`'s "Add transaction" sheet. Takes an optional existing `SpendTransaction` (nil = create, non-nil = edit) so one view serves both, per STANDARDS §3 no-copy-paste.
  - `MerchantRulesView.swift` — rule list.
  - `MerchantRuleEntryView.swift` — add/edit rule form; on save, invokes the retag pass described above.
- Wire into `Reservoir/App/RootTabView.swift`: replace the placeholder `Text("Transactions")` with `TransactionsView()`.
- Wire into `Reservoir/Features/Today/TodayView.swift`: replace the `StubSheet` in `.sheet(isPresented: $isShowingAddTransaction)` with `TransactionEntryView()` (create mode, no goal preselected beyond the same active-goal default logic described above — reuse, don't reimplement).
- Any non-trivial logic here (rule matching against merchant name, retroactive retag pass, "is this the sole active goal" default, date-bound validation) should be pure functions in `Services/`, not inlined in the views, per STANDARDS §3/§4 — likely a small `TransactionEntryValidator` or similar (naming per engineer's judgment, following the `*Calculator`/`*Validator` suffix convention in STANDARDS §4). Flag to engineer: given the goal-default-picking logic already exists as `TodayScreenCalculator.activeGoals`, reuse that rather than re-deriving "active goal" logic in a second place.
- The merchant-name-match function (`[MerchantRule] -> String -> TransactionType?`) and the retag-application function (takes matched rule + existing transactions, returns which to update) should both be pure, testable `Services/` types — `MerchantRule` CRUD (create/edit/delete themselves) is otherwise straightforward `@Model` mutation via `modelContext`, no new pure-logic type needed for the CRUD mechanics alone.
- Flag to engineer: adq.4 depends on the matching function built here. Name/expose it so it's reusable from a different feature folder (e.g. don't bury it as a private type inside `TransactionEntryView.swift`).
- **Kickoff checks added 2026-07-12 (engineering review pass), confirm or flag back before/while implementing — none block starting, but resolve rather than discover mid-build:**
  1. Verify `TodayScreenCalculator.activeGoals` is actually a standalone, non-private `[SavingsGoal] -> [SavingsGoal]`-shaped pure function (or trivially extractable to one) before assuming it's reusable as-is from the new `Transactions` feature folder. If it's coupled to `TodayScreenCalculator`'s instance state/today's-date context rather than a plain filter, do a small extraction refactor rather than re-deriving "active goal" logic in a second place.
  2. The retag pass (rule save + batch transaction updates) should be a single atomic `modelContext.save()` after all mutations (rule + retagged transactions), not two sequential saves — a partial failure (rule saved, retag not applied) would leave the "immediately retags" contract broken.
  3. The retag pass must not re-fire on a MerchantRule edit that changes neither `merchantName` nor `type` (a no-op save). Diff old vs. new rule state inside the same pure retag-application function (so it's covered by the same unit tests) rather than leaving "did anything change" logic to the view layer.
  4. No background task/async plumbing needed for the retag pass — single-user, synchronous, main-actor SwiftData save is sufficient; don't over-engineer concurrency handling here.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Transactions tab replaces the placeholder `Text("Transactions")` in `RootTabView` and shows a day-grouped, date-descending list of all `SpendTransaction`s.
- [ ] Each row shows type icon, merchant, time, amount, goal attribution (or "Unattributed"), and fixed transactions render muted with "Excluded from limit," matching `TodayView`'s existing visual convention.
- [ ] Empty state (no transactions) shows a message, not a blank list.
- [ ] All/Variable/Fixed filter control works and is covered by an XCUITest.
- [ ] "+" on the Transactions tab and "Add transaction" on the Today screen both open the same real `TransactionEntryView` (Today's stub sheet removed).
- [ ] Entry form validates: amount > 0; date <= today (device local, `Calendar.current`); merchant name non-empty after trim. Invalid input blocks save and shows an inline, field-level error (not a generic alert) — covered by unit tests for the validation logic and at least one XCUITest exercising a validation failure end to end.
- [ ] Goal attribution picker: auto-selects the sole active goal when exactly one exists; otherwise requires an explicit choice (including "Unattributed") before save is enabled. Covered by a unit test on the selection-default logic.
- [ ] Merchant-name match against existing `MerchantRule`s auto-fills `type` on the entry form (case-insensitive exact match), user can still override, and overriding sets `isManualOverride = true`. Covered by a unit test on the match/override logic.
- [ ] **(Added 2026-07-12, product review pass)** When no `MerchantRule` matches the entered merchant name, the entry form defaults to `type = variable` and `isManualOverride = false` — covered by a unit test asserting this no-match default explicitly (previously only the match/override path had a stated test).
- [ ] **(Added 2026-07-12, product review pass)** With zero active `SavingsGoal`s, the goal-attribution picker on the Transactions tab's "+" entry (reachable even with no goals, unlike Today's) renders "Unattributed" pre-selected with no goal list to choose from, and save proceeds without requiring further input.
- [ ] **Retroactive retag**: creating or editing a MerchantRule (name or type change) immediately retags all existing `SpendTransaction`s with a case-insensitive matching `merchantName` and `isManualOverride == false` to the rule's `type`; transactions with `isManualOverride == true` are left untouched. Covered by a unit test with a mixed fixture (some matching transactions manually overridden, some not) asserting only the non-overridden ones change. Deleting a rule does not retroactively change any transaction.
- [ ] The merchant-match function and the retag-application function are pure `Services/` functions/types (not inlined in views), unit tested, and structured so reservoir-adq.4 can call the match function directly for its import-time use case without reimplementing it.
- [ ] Editing an existing transaction (including reassigning its goal) persists correctly; deleting requires confirmation and removes the record.
- [ ] Merchant rules list (create/edit/delete) is reachable from the Transactions tab, rejects duplicate merchant names (case-insensitive) with an inline error, and requires an explicit type selection on create (no silent default).
- [ ] Business logic (validation, goal-default selection, merchant-match lookup, retag pass) lives in `Services/` as SwiftUI-free pure functions/types, unit tested to STANDARDS §5's 80% bar.
- [ ] XCUITest coverage for: add a transaction end to end, edit a transaction, delete a transaction, create/edit/delete a merchant rule, and creating a rule that retroactively retags at least one existing transaction visible in the list.
- [ ] README updated (STANDARDS §5/§6) — this story adds a real feature (Transactions tab, MerchantRule UI, retroactive retag behavior) replacing a stub, which is exactly the "features changed" trigger for a README update.
- [ ] Commits scoped `feat(transactions)` / `feat(merchant-rules)` per STANDARDS §2 (matches the feature areas this story defines — use `merchant-rules` as its own scope since it's a distinct enough surface, engineer's call if a single `transactions` scope covers both better in practice).
notes:
Open product questions flagged for JP (not decided unilaterally — everything else in this story's description reflects a call I made and justified inline):

1. **RESOLVED 2026-07-07** — Does creating/editing a MerchantRule retroactively re-tag existing transactions? JP decided: yes. Retroactive re-tagging of existing non-manually-overridden transactions is now built into this story (adq.3), triggered synchronously on MerchantRule create/edit. adq.4 is narrowed to import-time application of the same matching function only. See this story's "Scope note" section and acceptance criteria for the full split.

2. **Deliberately deferred, not dropped** — should the manual-entry form's auto-suggest and the retroactive-retag-on-rule-save pass share one literal matching implementation, or is it acceptable if they happen to duplicate the same logic for now? JP said to ignore this for now. Documented as a known open issue in this story's description ("Known open issue, deliberately deferred") — engineer should default to one shared pure function for both call sites since that satisfies the requirement either way, but the explicit product decision on this is still outstanding. STILL OPEN as of 2026-07-12 refinement pass — not resolved in this pass either, per explicit instruction not to touch it.

3. **RESOLVED 2026-07-12** — Should `MerchantRule` carry a default `SavingsGoal` for auto-attribution (requiring a SchemaV3 migration)? JP decided: no. Do not add a `defaultGoal` field; proceed with adq.3 as scoped (goal attribution stays manual, per-transaction). Reasoning: goal attribution only matters once 2+ SavingsGoals are simultaneously active (each has independent, non-pooled carry-forward); PROJECT_SPEC.md marks multi-goal support as Phase 2, not MVP, so the premise this question depends on isn't guaranteed during MVP usage. A per-merchant default goal is also a fairly specific usage pattern that may not match how JP actually uses multiple goals if/when he has them. Revisit only if JP runs 2+ goals concurrently and finds manual attribution genuinely annoying in practice. See description's "Data model" section for the pointer, and the 2026-07-12 comment for full reasoning.

## 2026-07-12 refinement pass (product-lead + engineer review, orchestrator-run)

Story reviewed for completeness/consistency against docs/PROJECT_SPEC.md and STANDARDS.md before engineering starts. Verdict from both reviewers: ready for engineering, with the following small additions folded into the description/acceptance criteria (not re-specs, just closing gaps):

- Added AC: no-match default (`variable`, `isManualOverride = false`) needs its own explicit unit test, not just the match/override path.
- Added UX clarification + AC: zero-active-goals state for the Transactions tab's independent "+" entry (reachable with zero goals, unlike Today's) — picker renders "Unattributed" pre-selected, no goal list, no blocking confirm needed.
- Added technical clarifications to "Technical approach": verify `TodayScreenCalculator.activeGoals` is actually a reusable standalone pure function before assuming import (small extraction refactor may be needed if it's coupled to the view); retag pass should be one atomic `modelContext.save()` covering rule + retagged transactions, not two sequential saves; retag pass must not fire on a no-op MerchantRule edit (diff old vs. new state inside the pure retag-application function); no background task/async needed for the retag pass (single-user, synchronous, main-actor is sufficient).
- No new open product questions surfaced by this pass. Question 2 remains open exactly as before (untouched, per instruction). Question 3 is now resolved (see above).
- Nothing flagged as scope creep or under-scoped beyond the two AC additions above; category-field and search exclusions were re-confirmed as correct calls.

title: Move Plaid OAuth universal link off johnpease.github.io to a dedicated domain
description: The apple-app-site-association file for Plaid's OAuth-institution redirect currently lives in a new johnpease.github.io user-pages repo (created 2026-07-14 as a workaround since GitHub Pages for a project repo can't serve at the domain root Apple requires for Associated Domains). This ties the app's OAuth capability to JP's personal GitHub-account root domain, which also means any other future use of johnpease.github.io (e.g. a personal site/portfolio) would need to coexist with or replace this AASA file. Move to a dedicated domain (or subdomain) JP controls once one exists, update: the AASA file's host, Reservoir.entitlements' associated-domains entry (project.yml), PlaidOAuthRedirect.url in PlaidServiceLive.swift, and the Allowed redirect URI in the Plaid dashboard.

title: GoalsScreenUITests.testCreateGoalStartDateInFutureShowsError hardcodes wall-clock 'today', flakes as date advances
description: ReservoirUITests/GoalsScreenUITests.swift:142 hardcodes a specific day-of-month intended to mean 'tomorrow' relative to when the test was written, assuming a fixed wall-clock 'today'. It has now flaked twice for this exact reason: once when today advanced from July 8 to July 9 (noted during the PR #5 first fix-commit pass), and again when today advanced to July 10 (noted during the reservoir-1et banked-surplus fix verification). The test doesn't mock/inject 'today' the way GoalsScreenCalculatorTests and TodayScreenCalculatorTests do (both use an injected reference date) — it relies on the simulator's live clock. Fix: compute the target date relative to Date() at test-run time (e.g. calendar.date(byAdding: .day, value: 1, to: .now)) instead of a hardcoded day-of-month, so the test doesn't depend on which day it happens to run.
close_reason: Fixed in commit 2c0349e (feature/goals-screen, PR #5): testCreateGoalStartDateInFutureShowsError now computes its target date via calendarOffset(forDaysFromToday:) relative to Date() at test-run time instead of a hardcoded day-of-month. Verified: full suite 139/139 pass.

title: Add test coverage for Simulation completion outcome when goal is behind pace and due today
description: Follow-up from the Simulation completion-date fix (commit 2309005, PR #5 / reservoir-adq.5). GoalsScreenCalculator.completionOutcome now has an explicit daysRemaining==0 + avgDailyNet>0 case (returns .onSchedule) with a test. The avgDailyNet<0 (behind pace) branch combined with daysRemaining==0 (goal due today, already behind) was not touched by that fix and remains untested — not a known regression, but an untested edge case worth locking down with a unit test asserting the late-completion date/day count is sane when the goal is due today.
close_reason: Added testSimulationLateCompletionWhenTargetDateIsTodayAndBehindPace in commit 4c94b13, confirms the untouched .late branch stays correct at daysRemaining==0

title: Cache CompletionBannerView.isGoalMet to avoid recompute on every body access
description:
TodayView.swift CompletionBannerView.isGoalMet (~line 360) is a computed property that re-runs the full carryForwardInput mapping (goal.transactions.map over all spend entries) plus DailyLimitCalculator's day-by-day carry-forward loop every time it's accessed. It's accessed at least twice per body render (icon selection at line 366, copy branch at line 369), so the full calculation runs twice per render.

Flagged during code review of PR #4 (feature/goal-met-verification, reservoir-4za). Not currently impactful — single-user app, low transaction volume per goal — but worth fixing for hygiene/scalability. Fix: compute isGoalMet once (e.g. as a 'let' in body, or backing @State set in .onAppear/.task) and reuse the cached value for both the icon and copy branch, rather than re-deriving it on each access.
